title: LoViF 2026 The Challenge on Ultra-Low Bitrate Image Compression @ ECCV 2026
description: Our goal is to introduce a new benchmark for ultra-low bitrate image compression under extreme bitrate constraints.
description_zh: 我们的目标是在极端比特率约束下，为超低比特率图像压缩引入一个新的基准。

pages.title: overview
pages.content:
The 2nd Workshop on Low-level Vision Frontiers (LoViF) @ ECCV 2026

 

Challenge on Ultra-Low Bitrate Image Compression

Important dates

  • 2026.05.27 — Release of the train data
  • 2026.05.27 — Release of the validation data
  • 2026.05.27 — Validation server online
  • 2026.07.08 — Deadline for code and checkpoint submission
  • 2026.07.09 — Release of the final test data
  • 2026.07.14 — Deadline for test result submission
  • 2026.07.14 — Deadline for factsheet submission
  • 2026.07.21 — Preliminary test results released to participants
  • 2026.07.21 — Workshop paper submission deadline
  • 2026.09.xx — LoViF workshop and challenges, results and award ceremony (ECCV 2026)

Challenge overview

The 2nd Workshop on Low-level Vision Frontiers with Generative AI, Preference Optimization and Agentic Systems (LoViF) will be held in September 2026 in conjunction with ECCV 2026.

Image compression is undergoing rapid evolution toward more perceptually oriented visual coding paradigms. Traditional codecs mainly focus on pixel-wise fidelity, which often leads to severe blurring, blocking artifacts, and loss of fine textures at ultra-low bitrates. Recently, advanced learning-based approaches, especially those leveraging powerful generative priors, have demonstrated strong potential in synthesizing perceptually realistic details and improving visual quality under extremely limited bitrate budgets.

This challenge focuses on ultra-low bitrate image compression, aiming to push the boundaries of perceptual reconstruction, visual quality, and compact visual representations under extreme bitrate constraints. Participants are encouraged to explore innovative compression frameworks, including approaches that incorporate advanced generative priors (e.g., diffusion models, GANs, autoregressive models, etc.), as well as other advanced learning-based techniques. We welcome researchers and practitioners from both academia and industry to benchmark their latest algorithms and contribute to the development of next-generation image compression systems.

Motivation for this competition

While classic image compression has achieved high coding efficiency, traditional methods often suffer from severe blurring, blocking, and texture degradation at ultra-low bitrates. This is largely because standard optimization metrics (such as PSNR and MS-SSIM) prioritize pixel-wise fidelity over perceptual realism, which may not fully align with human visual preferences.

Recent advances in learning-based image generation have opened new opportunities for ultra-low bitrate compression. In particular, generative priors and perceptually driven reconstruction strategies have shown strong capability in restoring realistic textures and improving visual quality from highly compact representations. Nevertheless, balancing perceptual quality, semantic consistency, and coding efficiency under extremely low bitrate constraints remains a challenging problem.

To promote research in this area, the LoViF Challenge introduces a specialized benchmark focused on ultra-low bitrate image compression. We encourage participants to explore advanced perceptual reconstruction techniques and innovative coding strategies that improve human visual experience while maintaining competitive compression performance. Ultimately, this competition aims to establish a practical and forward-looking benchmark for next-generation visual coding systems.

Top-ranked participants will be awarded and invited to submit workshop papers describing their methods and solutions to the associated LoViF workshop at ECCV 2026.

More details are available in the competition data and evaluation sections.

Awards

The following awards will be provided to the top-performing teams:

  • One First-Class Award (Champion): USD $1,000
  • One Second-Class Award: USD $600
  • One Third-Class Award: USD $400

Provided Resources

  • Scripts: The organizers will provide standard evaluation scripts to facilitate the reproducibility of the images and performance evaluation results. Please check the data page for download links and instructions.
  • Contact: Please use the forum on the challenge page for technical discussions, or directly contact the challenge organizers by email (xiaoyue_ling@sjtu.edu.cn and zhouchuqin@sjtu.edu.cn).
pages.content_zh:
第2届低阶视觉前沿工作坊(LoViF) @ ECCV 2026

 

超低比特率图像压缩挑战赛

重要日期

  • 2026.05.27 — 训练数据发布
  • 2026.05.27 — 验证数据发布
  • 2026.05.27 — 验证服务器上线
  • 2026.07.08 — 代码和检查点提交截止日期
  • 2026.07.09 — 最终测试数据发布
  • 2026.07.14 — 测试结果提交截止日期
  • 2026.07.14 — 事实表提交截止日期
  • 2026.07.21 — 初步测试结果发布给参赛者
  • 2026.07.21 — 工作坊论文提交截止日期
  • 2026.09.xx — LoViF工作坊和挑战赛、成果发布及颁奖典礼(ECCV 2026)

挑战赛概述

第2届低阶视觉前沿工作坊(涵盖生成式AI、偏好优化和智能体系统)(LoViF)将于2026年9月与ECCV 2026同期召开。

图像压缩正朝着更具感知导向的视觉编码范式快速发展。传统编码器主要关注逐像素保真度，这在超低比特率下往往导致严重的模糊、分块伪影和细节纹理丧失。最近，先进的基于学习的方法，特别是那些利用强大生成式先验的方法，在合成感知上真实的细节和改进极度受限比特率预算下的视觉质量方面展现了强大潜力。

本挑战赛聚焦超低比特率图像压缩，旨在突破极端比特率约束下的感知重建、视觉质量和紧凑视觉表示的边界。鼓励参赛者探索创新压缩框架，包括融合先进生成式先验(如扩散模型、GAN、自回归模型等)的方法，以及其他先进的基于学习的技术。我们欢迎来自学术界和工业界的研究人员和从业者基准测试其最新算法，为下一代图像压缩系统的发展做出贡献。

竞赛动机

虽然传统图像压缩已取得高编码效率，但传统方法在超低比特率下往往遭受严重模糊、分块和纹理退化问题。这主要是因为标准优化指标(如PSNR和MS-SSIM)将逐像素保真度优先于感知真实性，这可能无法充分符合人类视觉偏好。

近期学习型图像生成技术的进展为超低比特率压缩开辟了新机遇。特别是，生成式先验和感知驱动重建策略在从高度紧凑表示恢复真实纹理和改进视觉质量方面表现出强大能力。然而，在极低比特率约束下平衡感知质量、语义一致性和编码效率仍然是一个具有挑战的问题。

为推进该领域研究，LoViF挑战赛引入了一个专门针对超低比特率图像压缩的基准。我们鼓励参赛者探索先进的感知重建技术和创新编码策略，在保持竞争力压缩性能的同时改进人类视觉体验。最终，本竞赛旨在为下一代视觉编码系统建立一个实用且前瞻性的基准。

pages.title: data
pages.content:
The 2nd Workshop on Low-level Vision Frontiers (LoViF) @ ECCV 2026

 

Challenge on Ultra-Low Bitrate Image Compression

Data overview

The dataset for this challenge is constructed from selected samples of open-source high-resolution image datasets and high-quality images collected from the Internet. It is specifically curated to benchmark the frontiers of ultra-low bitrate image compression, featuring diverse real-world scenes with high-quality visual content. In this competition, the dataset is composed of:

  • train data: A total of 14,136 high-quality images are provided for training image compression models. Participants are encouraged to use this dataset. Additional public datasets are permitted, but all external data sources must be clearly disclosed in the factsheet.
  • validation data: A total of 100 high-quality images are provided for performance tuning during the Development Phase.
  • test data: A total of 100 high-quality images are additionally provided for the final evaluation during the Testing Phase.

Data access

By accessing the data, the participants implicitly agree with the terms and conditions of the challenge.

  • Training Data:
    • Official Training Dataset (14,136 high-quality images)
  • Development Phase:
    • Validation Data (100 high-quality images)
  • Testing Phase:
    • Test Data: To be released when the Testing Phase begins.

Development phase submission guidelines

Please follow the submission format described in the evaluation page when submitting your results on this website. The system will automatically evaluate and rank the submissions.


Code submission phase submission guidelines

After the development phase ends, teams intending to participate in the final evaluation during the test phase must submit their executable/source code and pretrained model checkpoints via email to the challenge organizers at: xiaoyue_ling@sjtu.edu.cn or zhouchuqin@sjtu.edu.cn. Please note that participants are only allowed to use the code and pretrained model checkpoints submitted above for the test phase evaluation.

The email subject should follow the format:

[LoViF2026] - [Ultra-Low Bitrate Image Compression] - [YOUR_TEAM_NAME]

The email body must include the following information:

  1. Full challenge name: LoViF 2026 - Challenge on Ultra-Low Bitrate Image Compression
  2. Team name
  3. Team leader’s name and email address
  4. Team name and usernames registered on the LoViF 2026 Codabench server
  5. Attached executable/source code and pretrained models, or corresponding download links

The executable/source code package must include all trained models and necessary parameters to ensure reproducibility. A README file describing how to run the code is mandatory.


Final test phase submission guidelines ( Factsheet can be found in here )

The final submission should also be emailed to the challenge organizers at: xiaoyue_ling@sjtu.edu.cn or zhouchuqin@sjtu.edu.cn.

The email subject should follow the format:

[LoViF2026] - [Ultra-Low Bitrate Image Compression] - [YOUR_TEAM_NAME]

The email body must include the following information:

  1. Full challenge name: LoViF 2026 - Challenge on Ultra-Low Bitrate Image Compression
  2. Team name
  3. Team leader’s name and email address
  4. Names, email addresses, and affiliations/institutions of all team members
  5. Team name and usernames registered on the LoViF 2026 Codabench server
  6. Attached Factsheet (Please use the provided LoViF template. In addition, the Factsheet submission must include both the compiled PDF version and the corresponding LaTeX source files.)
  7. Download links to the FULL results of ALL test frames (Please follow the submission format described in the evaluation page)

Final test phase - preliminary results [TBA]

Preliminary results will be posted here once the evaluation is complete.

Note that top-ranked participants will be invited to co-author the challenge report and contribute a paper to the LoViF 2026 workshop. All challenge papers will undergo a peer-review process. For more information, please visit the LoViF 2026 Official Website .
pages.content_zh:
# 第2届低级视觉前沿研讨会 (LoViF) @ ECCV 2026

## 超低比特率图像压缩挑战赛

### 数据概览

该挑战赛的数据集由开源高分辨率图像数据集和从互联网收集的高质量图像精选样本构成。它专门为超低比特率图像压缩的前沿基准测试而精心策划，具有多样化的真实场景和高质量的视觉内容。在本次竞赛中，数据集包括：

- **训练数据：** 提供14,136张高质量图像用于训练图像压缩模型。鼓励参赛者使用该数据集。允许使用额外的公开数据集，但所有外部数据源必须在信息说明书中清楚披露。

- **验证数据：** 在开发阶段提供100张高质量图像用于性能调整。

- **测试数据：** 在测试阶段额外提供100张高质量图像用于最终评估。

### 数据访问

**参赛者访问数据即表示隐性同意本挑战赛的条款和条件。**

- **训练数据：**
  - [官方训练数据集](https://huggingface.co/datasets/happyk/image_data/resolve/main/dataset_train.zip)（14,136张高质量图像）

- **开发阶段：**
  - [验证数据](https://huggingface.co/datasets/happyk/image_data/resolve/main/dataset_val.zip)（100张高质量图像）

- **测试阶段：**
  - 测试数据：将在测试阶段开始时发布

### 开发阶段提交指南

提交结果时，请遵循评估页面中描述的提交格式。系统将自动评估并排名提交的作品。

### 代码提交阶段提交指南

开发阶段结束后，意向在测试阶段进行最终评估的团队必须通过电子邮件向挑战赛组织者提交其可执行文件/源代码和预训练模型检查点：
[xiaoyue_ling@sjtu.edu.cn](mailto:xiaoyue_ling@sjtu.edu.cn) 或 [zhouchuqin@sjtu.edu.cn](mailto:zhouchuqin@sjtu.edu.cn)

**请注意，参赛者只允许使用上述提交的代码和预训练模型检查点进行测试阶段评估。**

电子邮件主题应遵循以下格式：

**[LoViF2026] - [超低比特率图像压缩] - [您的团队名称]**

电子邮件正文必须包含以下信息：

1. 完整的挑战赛名称：LoViF 2026 - 超低比特率图像压缩挑战赛

2. 团队名称

3. 团队领导者的姓名和电子邮件地址

4. 在LoViF 2026 Codabench服务器上注册的团队名称和用户名

5. 附加的可执行文件/源代码和预训练模型，或对应的下载链接

可执行文件/源代码包必须包含所有经过训练的模型和必要的参数，以确保可重现性。必须包含一个描述如何运行代码的README文件。

### 最终测试阶段提交指南（信息说明书见）

pages.title: evaluation
pages.content:
The 2nd Workshop on Low-level Vision Frontiers (LoViF) @ ECCV 2026

 

Challenge on Ultra-Low Bitrate Image Compression

Evaluation

The evaluation consists of comparing the reconstructed/generated images with the reference ground truth images. To comprehensively evaluate ultra-low bitrate image compression performance, both fidelity-oriented and perception-oriented metrics will be considered, including PSNR, MS-SSIM, LPIPS, DISTS, and bitrate (BPP).

The bitrate (BPP) will be computed directly from the submitted bitstreams (.bin files), while distortion and perceptual quality metrics will be evaluated using the submitted reconstructed PNG images. Both PSNR and MS-SSIM are computed in the RGB domain.

Bitstream Constraints

  • This challenge only requires participants to submit results at one single bitrate operating point. Bitstream files must use the .bin extension.
  • The average bitrate constraint is: average_bpp ≤ 0.008. Submissions violating the bitrate constraint will be considered invalid and will not appear on the leaderboard.

Final Score

The final ranking will be determined by the following weighted score:

Final_Score = PSNR + 10 × MS_SSIM + 20 × (1 - LPIPS) + 25 × (1 - DISTS)

Submission Format

For submitting the results, please follow the instructions below:

  1. Please ensure that the reconstructed output images have exactly the same filenames as the input images (example: for an input file named 0001.png, the reconstructed output image should also be named 0001.png). The reconstructed images must have exactly the same spatial resolution as the input images.
  2. Create a ZIP archive following the required submission structure: 

    submission.zip
│
├── reconstructed/
│    ├── 0001.png
│    ├── 0002.png
│
├── bitstream/
│    ├── 0001.bin
│    ├── 0002.bin
│
└── readme.txt
    The readme.txt file should contain the following lines filled in with the runtime per image (in seconds) of the solution, 1 or 0 accordingly if employs CPU or GPU at runtime, and 1 or 0 if employs extra data for training the models or not. 
    runtime per image [s] : 10.43
CPU[1] / GPU[0] : 1
Extra Data [1] / No Extra Data [0] : 1
Other description: Solution based on A+ of Timofte et al. ACCV 2014. We have a Matlab/C++ implementation, and report single core CPU runtime. The method was trained on Train 91 of Yang et al. and BSDS 200 of the Berkeley segmentation dataset.
    The last part of the file can have any description you want about the code producing the provided results (dependencies, link, scripts, etc.) The provided information is very important both during the validation period when different teams can compare their results / solutions but also for establishing the final ranking of the teams and their methods.

For more details regarding the LoViF workshop and related topics on Generative AI, Preference Optimization, and Agentic Systems, please visit the official workshop website: https://lovif-eccv2026-workshop.github.io/
pages.content_zh:
第2届低阶视觉前沿研讨会（LoViF）@ ECCV 2026

 

超低比特率图像压缩挑战赛

评估

评估过程包括将重建/生成的图像与参考真实图像进行比较。为了全面评估超低比特率图像压缩性能，将采用保真度导向和感知导向的指标，包括PSNR、MS-SSIM、LPIPS、DISTS和比特率（BPP）。

比特率（BPP）将直接从提交的比特流（.bin文件）中计算，而失真和感知质量指标将使用提交的重建PNG图像进行评估。PSNR和MS-SSIM均在RGB域中计算。

比特流约束

  • 本挑战赛要求参赛者仅在一个单一比特率工作点提交结果。比特流文件必须使用.bin扩展名。
  • 平均比特率约束为： average_bpp ≤ 0.008。违反比特率约束的提交将被视为无效，不会出现在排行榜上。

最终得分

最终排名由以下加权分数确定：

Final_Score = PSNR + 10 × MS_SSIM + 20 × (1 - LPIPS) + 25 × (1 - DISTS)

提交格式

请按照以下说明提交结果：

  1. 请确保重建输出图像的文件名与输入图像完全相同（例如：对于名为0001.png的输入文件，重建输出图像也应命名为0001.png）。重建的图像必须与输入图像具有完全相同的空间分辨率。
  2. 创建遵循所需提交结构的ZIP压缩包： 

    submission.zip
│
├── reconstructed/
│    ├── 0001.png
│    ├── 0002.png
│
├── bitstream/
│    ├── 0001.bin
│    ├── 0002.bin
│
└── readme.txt
    readme.txt文件应包含以下行，填入解决方案的每图像运行时间（以秒为单位）、是否在运行时使用CPU或GPU（1或0）、以及是否在训练模型时使用额外数据（1或0）。 
    runtime per image [s] : 10.43
CPU[1] / GPU[0] : 1
Extra Data [1] / No Extra Data [0] : 1
Other description: Solution based on A+ of Timofte et al. ACCV 2014. We have a Matlab/C++ implementation, and report single core CPU runtime. The method was trained on Train 91 of Yang et al. and BSDS 200 of the Berkeley segmentation dataset.
    文件的最后部分可以包含有关代码和所提供结果的任何描述（依赖项、链接、脚本等）。 这些提供的信息在验证期间非常重要，此时不同团队可以比较他们的结果/解决方案，同时也有助于建立团队和方法的最终排名。

有关LoViF研讨会和生成式AI、偏好优化和智能体系统等相关主题的更多详情，请访问官方研讨会网站： https://lovif-eccv2026-workshop.github.io/

pages.title: organizers
pages.content:
The 2nd Workshop on Low-level Vision Frontiers (LoViF) @ ECCV 2026

 

Organizers

 

The LoViF Challenge on Ultra-Low Bitrate Image Compression is organized as part of the 2nd Workshop on Low-level Vision Frontiers with Generative AI, Preference Optimization and Agentic Systems (LoViF) and the LoViF 2026 workshop. The challenge results will be presented during the LoViF 2026 workshop and included in the associated ECCV 2026 workshop proceedings.

For any questions regarding the challenge, please contact the organizers of the LoViF Ultra-Low Bitrate Image Compression challenge:

  • Xiaoyue Ling: xiaoyue_ling@sjtu.edu.cn
  • Chuqin Zhou: zhouchuqin@sjtu.edu.cn

For more information about the LoViF workshop and organizers, please visit: https://lovif-eccv2026-workshop.github.io/
pages.content_zh:
第2届低层次视觉前沿研讨会(LoViF) @ ECCV 2026

 

组织者

 

LoViF超低比特率图像压缩挑战赛是作为第2届低层次视觉前沿研讨会及生成式人工智能、偏好优化和智能体系统研讨会(LoViF)的一部分而组织的，该挑战赛与LoViF 2026研讨会相关联。挑战赛结果将在LoViF 2026研讨会期间展示，并收录在相关的ECCV 2026研讨会论文集中。

如对挑战赛有任何疑问，请联系LoViF超低比特率图像压缩挑战赛的组织者：

  • 凌晓悦： xiaoyue_ling@sjtu.edu.cn
  • 周楚琴： zhouchuqin@sjtu.edu.cn

如需了解关于LoViF研讨会和组织者的更多信息，请访问： https://lovif-eccv2026-workshop.github.io/
